name: Chili-Corn Sauce - Master Chefs
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Onion, top and bottom cut
1 red Bell Pepper, top and bottom cut
1 green Bell Pepper, top and bottom cut
12 ounces Beef Stew Meat (shin, chuck, or round), cut into 1-inch cubes
½ cup Vermouth (dry) OR ½ cup White Wine
recipeInstructions:
Coarsely chop the reserved trimmings from your onions and peppers; set aside.
Cut the whole onion and peppers into a neat fine dice; set aside.
Using a small paring knife, remove the corn kernels from the cob, reserving the cobs. Combine the kernels with the diced vegetable mixture.
In a large heavy saucepan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil until very hot but not smoking. Add the meat cubes and stir over high heat, shaking the pan, until the meat is nicely browned (about 7 minutes).
Add the chopped vegetable trimmings to the meat and toss for 4 to 5 minutes.
Add the garlic to the mixture and toss for 2 minutes.
Add the chili powder, ground red pepper, and black pepper to the mixture and stir for 2 minutes.
Add the vermouth and reduce the mixture for 2 minutes.
Add the Veal Stock and reserved corn cobs. Bring the mixture to a boil, stirring frequently, until the stock has reduced to a light syrupy consistency (20 minutes or more).
Add the cream and continue to boil gently, skimming any fat from the surface, until the liquid coats the back of a spoon (about 8 minutes or longer).
Remove and discard the corn cobs.
Strain half of the mixture into a clean saucepan. Add the corn and vegetable mixture to the strained liquid and boil for 2 minutes. Set aside.
Combine the unstrained half and solids from the strained half and reserve as stew for another meal. Further cooking may be necessary.
Source: New York's Master Chefs, Bon Appetit Magazine
Written by Richard Sax, Photographs by Nancy McFarland
The Knapp Press, Los Angeles, 1985
Chef: Larry Forgione, An American Place Restaurant, New York
